Specifically, they establish that trial courts must apply the law correctly, consider all relevant evidence, and follow proper procedures, including discovery rules. They also suggest that prolonged separation without a hearing could potentially constitute an abuse of discretion or a violation of due process. In addition, they provide guidance on contempt standards and the handling of discovery violations.
